action.reasoning: Observation: val_eval.py crashed in tensor_to_rgba_pil with 'pic should not have > 4 channels. Got 256 channels' (renderer output dict likely has 'rgb' or other key with last dim=256 e.g. features/256-dim instead of 3; previous inspect showed [1,256,256,3] but key selection failed). Best LPIPS still 0.6021 (partial orbit fallback); custom per-target rays with rel_az + offset=180 gave 0.6124 (worse). Full val test succeeded but metrics poor due to possible azimuth convention offset or BG. Analysis: Root cause is fragile output handling in tensor_to_rgba_pil (wrong key picked from renderer dict, no fallback for >3 channels or different shapes like [B,C,H,W]). Zero-shot TripoSR with exact per-target pose (rel_az + offset) + white BG composite is still optimal approach (<1B params, no training, task-aligned). Val_eval sweep over azimuth_offsets will find best alignment (common offsets 0/90/180). Decision: Update val_eval.py with robust renderer output extraction (try keys ['comp_rgb','rgb','image','color'], take first 3 chans if >3, handle [B,C,H,W] vs [B,H,W,C], debug prints), sweep offsets [-180,180] step 30 on 20 objs vs GT targets using lpips+skimage for PSNR/SSIM, report best. Then port best offset+fixed func to predict.py, test full val, submit_eval for improved LPIPS.
